summary: “The searches that you’re doing on Claude require the consumption of energy and at some point the system should hit a breaking point,” says George Bilicic, Lazard vice chairman of investment banking and global head of power, energy and infrastructure. Bilicic joins Bloomberg Intelligence’s Alexandra Davidov and Rob Barnett on this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast to discuss why AI and data-center demand are reshaping the outlook for utilities, power markets and infrastructure investment. Moonshot AI’s release of Kimi K3 on Friday triggered a similar reaction, helping push semiconductor stocks sharply lower.

summary: Ghana has sentenced a senior opposition politician to 20 years in prison after convicting him on charges linked to illegal mining, Joy FM reported.

summary: Less than two years ago it looked like Louise Haigh’s political career was over. Now she’s the right-hand to UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham and central to his new government.

summary: Thames Water, the troubled UK utility that announced steep hikes in customer bills in recent years, is seeking another increase to fund a controversial reservoir in southeast England.

title: Hamas Picks Iran-Aligned Khalil Al-Hayya as Its Top Leader

summary: The European Union is set to discuss at least three possible scenarios for getting out of an impasse over its latest package of sanctions on Russia, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Trump Lawsuits Reach Key Stages After Pair of Recent Setbacks

title: India Court Ruling Extends Visa Uncertainty for Travelers
